---
title: 'Spiritual Impact on Natural Results - Haiti'
date: '2012-06-06T17:51:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '1753181920642241126'
blogger_filename: '/2012/06/spiritual-impact-on-natural-results.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Studies'
  - 'Prosperity'
  - 'Sensitive Issues'
  - 'News and Current Events'
---

<div style="text-align: justify;"><div style="text-align: center;"><b><a href="http://www.haiti.org/index.php?option=com_content&amp;view=frontpage&amp;Itemid=1" target="_blank">Haiti </a>- In The News</b></div></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br /></div><div style="text-align: justify;">Some time ago the world's news, especially the liberal press, went into violent outrage at the claim by Pat Robertson that Haiti experienced bad things because of it's ties to Satan. <a href="http://www.time.com/time/world/article/0,8599,1953959,00.html" rel="nofollow" target="_blank">TIME </a>magazine wrote an article regarding the differences between <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haiti" target="_blank">Haiti </a>and <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dominican_Republic#Religion" target="_blank">Dominican Republic</a> citing many natural causes for the countries differences. In response <a href="http://www.cbn.com/about/pressrelease_patrobertson_haiti.aspx" target="_blank">CBN put forth a response</a> statement to all of the outrage.<br /><br /><div style="text-align: center;"><b><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Religion_in_Haiti" target="_blank">Haiti- Religious</a> Background</b></div><br /><iframe allowfullscreen="" frameborder="0" height="200" src="http://www.youtube.com/embed/mtUlCJ0tLhI" style="clear: right; float: right; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-left: 1em;" width="300"></iframe>Haiti is a land of mixed religions. Catholics Christians, Protestant Christians, <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/West_African_Vodun" target="_blank">African&nbsp;</a>religions, Native Religions, even a few Muslims were brought in as slaves in the founding of this strange mixed up Island Country some 200+ years ago. Although the majority of the Island claims Christian Faith of some kind, it's more of a mix of Christianity, Native and <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/West_African_Vodun" target="_blank">African Religions</a> all put together. To this day over 50% of the&nbsp;population&nbsp;practices some form of&nbsp;<a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haitian_Vodou" target="_blank">Haitian&nbsp;Vodou</a>. For those unfamiliar with&nbsp;Vodou just think of the scene from <a href="http://youtu.be/mtUlCJ0tLhI" target="_blank">Pirates of the Caribbean "Tia Dalma"</a>. Vodou is OVERTLY Satanic in origin and practice. Secular people, with no Biblical foundation for truth, cannot understand the link between Satan and Vodou, although it is more than obvious with even a casual understanding of biblical history and who the devil is and what he represents.&nbsp;</div><br /><div style="text-align: center;"><b>Haiti - "Pact with the Devil"</b></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br />So where did this "Pact with Satan" come from, that Pat Robertson referred to? It refers to ceremony of "<a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bois_Ca%C3%AFman" target="_blank">Bois Caiiman</a>" led by a slave Vodou <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Houngan" target="_blank">Houngan </a>(priest) <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dutty_Boukman" target="_blank">Dutty Boukman</a>. He is described as a having a large warrior like appearance and a violent temper. This priest led a group of angry slaves in a ceremony.<br /><br />Wiki for <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bois_Ca%C3%AFman" target="_blank">Bois Caiiman</a> reads:<br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>A man named Boukman, another houngan, organized on August 14, 1791, a meeting with the slaves in the mountains of the North. This meeting took the form of a Voodoo ceremony in the Bois Caiman in the northern mountains of the island. It was raining and the sky was raging with clouds; the slaves then started confessing their resentment of their condition. A woman started dancing languorously in the crowd, taken by the spirits of the loas. With a knife in her hand, she cut the throat of a pig and distributed the blood to all the participants of the meeting who swore to kill all the whites on the island. On August 22, 1791, the blacks of the North entered into a rebellion, killing all the whites they met and setting the plantations of the colony on fire. However, the French quickly captured the leader of the slaves, Boukman, and beheaded him, bringing the rebellion under control. </i></blockquote>This ceremony was the start of the revolution which ultimately led to the people declaring their freedom and killing and burning their way to freedom. So in that sense the people who began this uprising indeed began it with roots in demonic ceremony. Root of an event has much to do with the fruit of an event. To this day over 1/2 of the population of Haiti still practices forms of Vodou.<br /><br />The&nbsp;slaves&nbsp;were right to be angry, as they had a right to be angry in the USA. However in the USA it was the Gospel of Jesus that led to the freedom of Slaves and eventually black equality. In Haiti it was a result of violent bloodshed and death. The blacks in the USA took longer to gain their freedom, but it was a lasting more peaceful freedom. This too could have come faster, but that is for another article.<br /><br /><div style="text-align: center;"><b>Dominican Republic - Religious Background</b></div><br />Like Haiti, the Dominican Republic is composed of a large number of Catholics and Protestants. Also like Haiti Vodou is practiced with subtle differences in style. The practices of the people who do practice Vodou are of a firm belief in One God. However this is were we see a difference. Haiti calls this God "Great Master" in a distant way. Dominican Republic calls this God "Good God".<br /><br /><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Religion_in_the_Dominican_Republic" target="_blank">Wiki Explains</a><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>Contrary to popular belief, like Haitian Voodun, practitioners of Dominican Vodou believe in one main God, the Creator, Papa Bon Dyé (the good God) as although physically absent from the Earth, he is still good to us all. In Haiti He also goes by Gran Met (the Great Master). The Kreyol influence in Dominican Vodou can be seen in the standard greeting used "Bonswa a la societé" </i></blockquote><div style="text-align: center;"><b>Dominican Republic - Origins: Freedom Through The Cross</b></div><br /><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/La_Trinitaria" target="_blank">La Trinitaria</a>, founded by <a href="http://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/605495/La-Trinitaria" target="_blank">Juan Pablo Duarte</a> on July 16th 1838 was a secret society formed to discuss and plan a declaration of&nbsp;independence&nbsp;of their half of the&nbsp;Island. <a href="http://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/605495/La-Trinitaria" target="_blank">The Flag</a> of the Dominican Republic was designed by these "Founding Fathers", and it contains a white Cross over a red and blue banner, symbolizing their Christian Heritage.<br /><br />Far from free, this land has many troubles of it's own. The same general people, language, occupations, hardships, and troubles have been consistent with both countries on the same Island. TIME magazine points to all of the natural reasons, geography, and social/political/language differences in the founding of these two wildly diverse nations. The UN Ranks the Dominican Republic 98 on their <a href="http://hdr.undp.org/en/statistics/" target="_blank">Human Development Index</a>. Haiti is ranks 158th. There are 187 Countries on their list.<br /><br /></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><div style="text-align: center;"><b>The Curse and The <a href="http://www.kcm.org/index.php?p=real_help_content&amp;id=1287" target="_blank">BLESSING</a></b></div></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br /></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><iframe allowfullscreen="" frameborder="0" height="200" src="http://www.youtube.com/embed/KjKAVSsUFh8" style="clear: right; float: right; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-left: 1em;" width="300"></iframe>Since the time of Adam and the fall of man the Curse has been living in this planet. The BLESSING that was in Adam to BLESS the whole earth was turned to a Curse when&nbsp;separated&nbsp;from it's source in the God of the BLESSING. Generally sin is under a curse and it is judged. Jesus came to restore the <a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+1%3A28&amp;version=KJV" target="_blank">BLESSING </a>of Adam, Noah, and Abraham and <a href="http://bible.cc/galatians/3-13.htm" target="_blank">redeem </a>us from the Curse. Since the time of Jesus the earth has lived under a dual leadership situation, much like the Island of Haiti. Half the the island is in&nbsp;absolute&nbsp;poverty and the other half, known as the <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dominican_Republic" target="_blank">Dominican Republic</a> is in&nbsp;relative&nbsp;abundance.<br /><br />So it is with the whole word. There are places that live in various manifestations of The BLESSING and there are nations that live in&nbsp;various&nbsp;manifestations of The Curse. No nation is ALL BLESSED or ALL CURSED. This is because the state of a people is&nbsp;dependent&nbsp;on the state of it's people. Any one person can live in The BLESSING. If they do they can begin to experience measures of the Garden of Eden in&nbsp;their&nbsp;lives. The more people that bring the BLESSING into a situation the greater it grows.<br /><br />Any direct act toward God as a group, no matter how small will bring His direct response to come in and protect them from the affects of the Curse. Any direct act against God and as any gentleman who is asked to leave would, He leaves. He removes His hand, partially or fully. His hand always brings the BLESSING. So when it's removed the Curse washes in.<br /><br />A nation that refuses to seek God, be they Haiti or the USA, or Israel itself will suffer from The Curse. This is not because God curses them. It is because the Curse already exists and it's waiting to enter in at any&nbsp;opportunity. This is what happened to Job. He engaged in sacrifices, not out of faith, but out of Fear. Fear is faith in evil's ability to harm you. Faith is released by c<a href="http://bible.cc/romans/10-9.htm" target="_blank">onfessing with the mouth and believing in the heart</a>. Therefore Job said: "..<a href="http://bible.cc/job/3-25.htm" target="_blank">.the thing I so greatly feared has come upon me.</a>.." Job allowed the curse to enter his life.<br /><br />Therefore what TIME doesn't, and can't, understand is that the natural things they cited are the RESULT of the BLESSING on Dominican Republic not the cause. All natural results are from spiritual causes. Even people who hate God, but do what He said and abide by His principles, will receive some natural results from that obedience. There are subtle differences between Haiti and Dominican Republic, but what set them apart was a different root.<br /><br /><div style="text-align: center;"><b>BLESSING or Curse</b></div><div style="text-align: center;"><b>-</b></div><div style="text-align: center;"><b>The Choice is Ours</b></div><div style="text-align: center;"><b><br /></b></div></div><div style="text-align: justify;">&nbsp;God told us in His word the <a href="http://bible.cc/deuteronomy/30-19.htm" target="_blank">BLESSING and Curse are ultimately our choice</a>. He told us what we could do that would bring us either response from the earth. The USA has been hit by&nbsp;catastrophes&nbsp;every time&nbsp;it's made a decision not to side with Israel. This is because God promised that those who <a href="http://bible.cc/genesis/12-3.htm" target="_blank">stand with Israel </a>would be BLESSED and those who stood against her would be Cursed.<br /><br />The wonderful thing in knowing this, is that there is never a moment that is too late. God's <a href="http://bible.cc/lamentations/3-23.htm" target="_blank">mercies are new</a> every morning. As any person, or nation, takes a stand and says: "God, I believe... what next?" God will lead them to their place in His plan. Haiti and the <a href="http://www.kcm.org/real-help/prophecy/what-about-2012" target="_blank">USA </a>are both in a similar position today. Both nations, at one level or another, have turned their back on God at a certain point in history. Haiti did this in 1791. The USA did this in 1947. Both have a <a href="http://www.kcm.org/real-help/prophecy/beginning-new-nation" target="_blank">promise from God</a> that they are reaching a turning point.<br /><br />Haiti will not end as they began. Haiti will rise and become the jewel of God she is meant to be. Even now, <a href="http://www.kcm.org/haiti/" target="_blank">Kenneth Copeland Ministries</a> has been and will be working in Haiti to deliver not just material support but spiritual support to this nation. Many Christians from all over the world and from within the nation have taken their place to stand with and in Haiti. There has already been great change. There is much work to be done. This end times world is in desperate need of the Gospel of Grace. We, The Church, will be and are rising. It's time to take our rightful place as ambassadors of The BLESSING and change a world in&nbsp;preparation&nbsp;for the rapture and the return.&nbsp;</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br /></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><div style="text-align: center;"><b>So I ask you two questions:</b></div><br /><ol><li>Are you working the BLESSING in your life?</li><li>Are you taking the BLESSING to the world around you?</li></ol></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><b>I call you empowered 2 prosper with good success!</b></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><b><br /></b></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><b>Darrell G. Wolfe</b></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><a href="http://n2gs.blogspot.com/">http://n2gs.blogspot.com/</a></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><a href="https://www.facebook.com/n2goodsuccess?sk=wall">https://www.facebook.com/n2goodsuccess</a></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><a href="http://empoweredtooprosper.blogspot.com/">http://empoweredtooprosper.blogspot.com/</a></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br /></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><b>See Also:</b></div><div style="text-align: justify;">Book Suggestion from the&nbsp;<a href="http://astore.amazon.com/your0f4d-20/190-6424964-9305846" target="_blank">N2 Good Success Amazon Store</a></div><div style="text-align: justify;">Product Offerings from&nbsp;<a href="http://www.zazzle.com/n2goodsuccess" target="_blank">N2 Good Success Zazzle Store</a></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br /></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><b>Bulldog Publications:&nbsp;</b></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><b><a href="http://www.amazon.com/dp/1453815619/ref=as_li_ss_til?tag=your0f4d-20&amp;camp=213381&amp;creative=390973&amp;linkCode=as4&amp;creativeASIN=1453815619&amp;adid=1NAWEWPYWDQA3XGCY7N5&amp;&amp;ref-refURL=http%3A%2F%2Fn2gs.blogspot.com%2F" target="_blank">Bankings Basics for Believers; Now Available on Amazon.&nbsp;</a></b></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><iframe frameborder="0" marginheight="0" marginwidth="0" scrolling="no" src="http://rcm.amazon.com/e/cm?lt1=_blank&amp;bc1=000000&amp;IS2=1&amp;bg1=FFFFFF&amp;fc1=000000&amp;lc1=0000FF&amp;t=your0f4d-20&amp;o=1&amp;p=8&amp;l=as4&amp;m=amazon&amp;f=ifr&amp;ref=ss_til&amp;asins=1453815619" style="height: 240px; width: 120px;"></iframe></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br /></div>
